6358.one_line_summary: Cross-chain token state synchronization protocol enabling global tokens synchronized across multiple blockchains.
6358.notes: Primary innovation is synchronization mechanism rather than token standard per se; defines consensus-agnostic cross-chain bridging at contract layer.

6366.one_line_summary: Permission token system using bitwise operations to represent authorization in an ecosystem.
6366.notes: Extends ERC-6617 to implement access control via uint256 permissions as alternative to ACLs; cross-ecosystem permission delegation.

6372.one_line_summary: Standardized interface for contracts to expose their internal clock value and tracking mechanism.
6372.notes: Improves composability by allowing external detection of block-number vs timestamp tracking; supports both native and cross-chain clocks.

6381.one_line_summary: Public emote repository enabling anyone to react to NFTs using standardized Unicode emojis.
6381.notes: Implements feedback-based NFT evolution and valuation via on-chain reactions; deterministic singleton contract for cross-chain compatibility.

6384.one_line_summary: Method to retrieve human-readable descriptions of EIP-712 typed data structures via contract.
6384.notes: Addresses phishing risk by allowing wallets to query verifying contract for message meaning; contract-controlled description prevents misrepresentation.

6454.one_line_summary: Minimal extension interface for ERC-721 to detect and enforce non-transferability of specific tokens.
6454.notes: Enables verifiable attribution and immutable properties by binding tokens to owners; avoids specifying state management mechanism.

6464.one_line_summary: Per-token, multi-operator approval extension for ERC-721 enabling fine-grained operator authorization.
6464.notes: Addresses setApprovalForAll security risk by allowing multiple specific operators per token with efficient bulk revocation via nonces.

6492.one_line_summary: Signature validation standard for predeploy contracts using wrapper format to verify counterfactual accounts.
6492.notes: Extends ERC-1271 to support signatures from contracts not yet deployed; wraps signature with deploy data and magic bytes for detection.

6506.one_line_summary: Escrow interface for peer-to-peer governance incentives enabling trustless vote bribing with verification.
6506.notes: Supports both on-chain and off-chain voting with optional dispute resolution; enables treasury buyback incentive mechanisms.

6538.one_line_summary: Canonical registry for stealth meta-addresses enabling private asset transfers via stealth addresses.
6538.notes: Singleton contract supporting EIP-712 and ERC-1271 signatures for delegation; interoperable with ERC-5564 stealth address generation.

6551.one_line_summary: Token-bound accounts system enabling NFTs to own assets and execute operations as smart contract accounts.
6551.notes: Deterministic registry using CREATE2 assigns smart accounts to NFTs; counterfactual accounts enable pre-deployment asset custody.

6596.one_line_summary: Comprehensive metadata standard for cultural and historical assets extending ERC-721/1155 with provenance and contextual fields.
6596.notes: 48 additional JSON properties for museums/institutions; supports search/discovery interoperability with IIIF and OAI protocols.

6604.one_line_summary: Abstract token standard enabling zero-cost off-chain minting with on-demand reification preserving on-chain composability.
6604.notes: Supports merkle trees and signatures; tokens exist as messages until reified on-chain; optional dereification for cross-chain/contract movement.

6617.one_line_summary: Bit-based permission and role system using uint256 bitwise operations for efficient access control.
6617.notes: Up to 256 permissions enabling 2^256 unique roles; supports importance ordering via bit position; optional metadata interface for descriptions.

6662.one_line_summary: ERC-4337 extension defining on-chain account metadata for user-friendly authentication via relay services.
6662.notes: Stores authenticator URIs and authentication schema on-chain; supports end-to-end encryption and signature aggregation for multisig.
